title: Arlingham (St. Augustine's Farm) via Gloucester / Beauchamp House

main_route.excerpt: We picked up Simon's 'cello, & drove on to __St. Augustine's__ working farm __Arlingham__.
Simon, Eve & Thomas heard Peter sing & play his violin in the orchestra at Beauchamp House.
drove on to __St. Augustine's__ working farm __Arlingham__. Peter & John slept in the [?bukta] & Kate slept on the bottom bunk.

unresolved.issue: Unclear word — possibly 'bukta' (a type of tent or sleeping arrangement), transcription uncertain; does not affect route.

boundary_flags: mixed_start: page A_0053_L also contains an August 4th entry (dropping Peter at Gloucester music camp, leaving cello for repair) which may belong to a separate earlier day-trip; only the August 10th overnight to Arlingham is treated as this trip.
mixed_end: page text ends mid-sentence ('& a half-'); subsequent content on following page not available.
summary: A short domestic trip on 10 August 1991 from Birmingham to Arlingham, Gloucestershire, stopping at Beauchamp House to watch Peter perform in the music-camp orchestra before arriving at St. Augustine's working farm to camp overnight. Family friend Kate Warburton joined the trip and babysat the younger children during the concert.
extraction_notes: Page A_0053_L is boundary-mixed. The August 4th entry records a separate day visit to Gloucester (drop Peter at music camp, leave cello for repair) — this is not the camping trip itself. The camping trip proper is the August 10th entry. Beauchamp House location is approximate; it hosted the music camp orchestra performance and is presumed to be in Gloucestershire near Gloucester, but no precise address is given. The word transcribed as '[?bukta]' is uncertain — possibly a brand/type of tent. No crossings; entirely within England.
